name: Porphyry sequence (granite · schist · ore)
expected_band: alternating granite/schist with ore veins; the CNN should beat the colour baseline on the ore

engine.model: procedural synthetic core-tray generator + run-merge segmentation (a sliding patch classifier merged into segments) + depth stitching; the same TS engine runs live in the browser and in the offline bake. The lithology CNN (torch->ONNX) runs live via onnxruntime-web; a classical colour/texture nearest-centroid model is the baseline.

honesty: The core-tray images are SYNTHETIC (procedural per-lithology textures), stated openly; UNIFORM/SHARP are closed-form analytic controls. The lithology CNN is measured against the classical colour/texture baseline (held-out accuracy) — a real ML-vs-features comparison, never a fabricated win. Low-confidence / out-of-distribution core is flagged, not forced into a class.
